nhs_cross_domain_fix: NHS edges reference IMM-POS-LAB-09 which should be IMM-POS-LAB-03 (care route closure)

edges.explanation: The 764k headline number conflating different categories is the primary evidence that route-specific analysis is required rather than blanket positions
Conflated headline figures directly support the claim that no blanket fiscal statement is honest
The scale of net migration makes the OBR fiscal finding politically difficult to communicate honestly
224,700 case backlog having quadrupled since 2014 demonstrates the processing failure driving costs
Quadrupled caseload with 61% post-refusal proves the system is slow not overwhelmed
£8m/day hotel cost is the direct fiscal consequence of processing failure that fast processing would eliminate
£8m/day hotel cost means 3-month processing pays for itself through reduced accommodation costs
Daily hotel costs prove the asylum system's expense is driven by slowness not volume
UK receiving fewer asylum applications per capita than EU average contradicts the narrative of being uniquely overwhelmed by asylum seekers
Below-EU-average application rates prove the system is slow not overwhelmed
£341k lifetime contribution per skilled migrant is the primary evidence for the positive fiscal case on the skilled worker route
Skilled migrants contributing £341k each makes zero net migration economically destructive
The scale of skilled migrant fiscal contribution is the finding no party honestly communicates
£19.9bn lower borrowing in high migration scenario proves zero net migration would devastate public finances
£19.9bn borrowing reduction is the specific OBR finding no party honestly communicates
OBR borrowing reduction confirms the aggregate positive fiscal case for higher migration
Family route costing -£109k per person proves fiscal impact is route-dependent, not uniformly positive
Negative fiscal impact on family route directly supports the claim that blanket statements are dishonest
0.6% depression at 5th percentile confirms wage suppression exists but is small relative to other factors
Only 3% of criminal deportation ECHR challenges succeeding proves the ECHR is a minor factor in deportation outcomes
Low ECHR success rate on criminal deportation supports the claim that ECHR exit is disproportionate
ECtHR issuing only 1 judgment against UK each year and granting 15/660 interim measures proves the Court is not blocking UK enforcement
Near-zero ECtHR interference proves leaving ECHR would not meaningfully increase deportation capacity
£700m spent on Rwanda with zero flights and no deterrence proves the scheme had no evidence base
Rwanda's failure at £700m vs Albania's success at bilateral cooperation proves targeted returns outperform performative deterrence
£700m with zero deportations contradicts the Conservative position of continuing Rwanda flights
Albanian arrivals dropping 90% after bilateral deal proves targeted cooperation works
Albanian model directly demonstrates the bilateral returns approach works in practice
1 in 5 NHS staff being foreign and 50% of workforce growth from overseas proves immigration sustains rather than strains the NHS
NHS dependence on overseas workers proves immigration dependency is a symptom of domestic underinvestment in training
Scale of NHS overseas workforce dependency is the evidence base for maintaining recruitment routes
Social care describing international recruitment as a lifeline while the route was closed proves immigration policy is harming not helping services
19% non-UK care workforce proves the domestic skills deficit that creates immigration dependency
Care worker route closure threatening extreme workforce shortages is the direct evidence for reinstating the route with standards
Closing the route while 19% of staff depend on it directly contradicts workforce stability
4.3 million home undersupply existing before migration increased proves housing pressure is a building failure not an immigration failure
One-third of productivity growth linked to migration-driven skills expansion proves zero migration would damage economic growth
Small boats being only 5% of total immigration proves the ECHR debate focuses on a tiny fraction of actual migration
Constitutional upheaval over 5% of immigration is by definition disproportionate
Route-specific fiscal finding is the analytical basis for the no-blanket-statement claim
Caps having no evidence of effectiveness is the basis for the MAC-led no-cap policy
ECHR being a minor factor supports the disproportionality claim
ECHR not blocking deportations means remaining in ECHR has negligible enforcement cost
Fast processing being dramatically cheaper proves the system is expensive because it's slow
The cost evidence is the direct justification for the 3-month processing target
Underfunding being the primary cause of service strain supports the domestic skills deficit diagnosis
Language being the strongest integration predictor directly justifies the day-one language provision policy
Speed of processing improving integration outcomes supports the right-to-work-after-3-months policy
Zero net migration being economically incoherent supports the evidence-based alternative of route-specific MAC management
Route-specific management only makes sense if the no-blanket-statement claim is correct
The immigration-skills pact depends on the claim that domestic underinvestment is the root cause
Maintaining overseas routes depends on the claim that immigration dependency reflects domestic failure not immigration excess
3-month processing depends on the claim that the system is slow not overwhelmed
Right to work after 3 months depends on fast processing being the right approach
Remaining in ECHR depends on the claim that exit is disproportionate to the problem
Binding annual cap directly contradicts the evidence-based no-cap MAC-led approach
IMA blocking all processing contradicts the fast processing approach and the Conservatives' own backlog target
Rwanda continuation contradicts the bilateral returns approach given £700m with zero flights
Banning care worker dependants directly contradicts maintaining overseas recruitment routes for social care
Labour's MAC-led skills linkage approach partially aligns with the evidence-based route-specific management
Labour's principle of sectors training domestically aligns with the skills pact but lacks enforcement mechanism
Border Security Command and scrapping Rwanda aligns closely with the bilateral cooperation enforcement approach
Labour closing the care worker route directly contradicts the policy to maintain overseas recruitment during the 15-year domestic transition
Lib Dems ending hostile environment and moving visa policy to economic department closely matches the evidence-based framing
Lib Dem firewall proposal directly matches the exploitation reporting policy
3-month processing target matches the evidence-based asylum processing policy exactly
Reversing care worker dependant bans partially supports maintaining overseas recruitment
Green no-restriction philosophy aligns directionally with evidence but the open borders aspiration has no evidence base for feasibility
Zero net migration and one-in-one-out directly contradict MAC-led route-specific management
Leaving ECHR and establishing a deportation command contradicts the remain-in-ECHR bilateral cooperation approach
Route-specific management creates the framework within which the immigration-skills pact operates
Fast processing is the prerequisite for the right-to-work policy — if claims are decided in 3 months, the right-to-work trigger is meaningful
Maintaining overseas routes and requiring domestic training co-investment create tension — sectors must recruit AND train simultaneously
Small wage effect at the bottom while immigration fills critical vacancies proves the problem is domestic pay policy not immigration
4.3 million home undersupply predating migration increase proves housing pressure is a domestic investment failure
One-third of productivity growth from migration-driven skills expansion is the economic case for maintaining managed migration
Small wage suppression in low-pay sectors supports requiring those sectors to co-fund domestic training rather than relying on low-wage overseas workers
Albanian deal working through bilateral cooperation not caps shows that targeted approaches outperform aggregate numerical restrictions
Evidence that safe routes reduce irregular crossings supports the remain-in-ECHR bilateral approach which includes expanded resettlement
Deterrence working only at disproportionate cost reinforces the claim that constitutional upheaval for enforcement is not justified
Albanian deal success partly driven by bilateral cooperation on returns integration, supporting the broader finding that targeted bilateral approaches aid integration
The route-specific management policy implicitly depends on honest communication of fiscal evidence which no party currently provides
The firewall policy depends on the finding that immigration is not the cause of service strain — the hostile environment punishes people who sustain services
Day-one language provision depends directly on the finding that language is the strongest predictor of integration success
Regional visa pathways depend on the claim that immigration dependency reflects domestic regional skills gaps

cross_domain_edges.explanation: The finding that underfunding not immigration causes service strain reinforces the NHS funding insufficiency claim
1 in 5 NHS staff being foreign directly supports the NHS workforce plan's dependency on overseas recruitment
Social care sector describing international recruitment as a lifeline supports the NHS social care funding and staffing policy
Reinstating care worker routes directly enables the NHS policy of maintaining overseas recruitment during the domestic transition
Immigration dependency being a domestic investment failure supports the claim that housing undersupply is a building failure not an immigration problem
£19.9bn lower borrowing from higher migration directly affects the fiscal space available for tax and spending policy
Small wage suppression at 5th percentile links to welfare policy on minimum wage and working conditions
